hoses: 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH between relay engines
appliances: Mounted deck gun on attack engine
sceneElements.elevation: No elevation for LDH relay questions; no elevation add-on for mounted deck gun

problems.title: Supply Engine Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 must deliver 500 GPM through 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH to the relay engine and maintain 20 PSI intake pressure at the relay engine. The LDH friction loss is 20 PSI and there is no elevation change. What discharge pressure should Engine 181 pump?
formulaBreakdown: Discharge pressure = LDH friction loss + desired intake pressure
problems.instructorExplanation: The supply engine must overcome 20 PSI LDH friction loss and still leave 20 PSI intake pressure at the relay engine.
problems.explainMistake: Do not stop at friction loss only. The receiving engine still needs 20 PSI intake pressure.

problems.title: Relay Engine Discharge Pressure
problems.question: The relay engine must deliver 500 GPM through 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH to the attack engine and maintain 20 PSI intake pressure at the attack engine. The LDH friction loss is 20 PSI and there is no elevation change. What discharge pressure should the relay engine pump?
formulaBreakdown: Discharge pressure = LDH friction loss + desired attack-engine intake pressure
problems.instructorExplanation: The relay engine must cover the LDH friction loss and maintain the requested intake pressure at the attack engine.
problems.explainMistake: Add the desired intake pressure to the relay hose friction loss.

problems.title: Attack Engine Deck Gun Pressure
problems.question: The attack engine is supplying a deck gun master stream at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. The deck gun is mounted on the attack engine, so do not add elevation for the height of the mounted deck gun. There is no hose friction loss or appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should the attack engine set?
formulaBreakdown: Mounted deck gun = part of the attack engine
PDP = NP + FL + elevation + appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: Do not add elevation for a deck gun mounted on the attack engine. With no hose or appliance losses, set the attack engine to the 80 PSI nozzle pressure.
problems.explainMistake: If you entered 90 PSI, you added 20 ft of deck-gun height as elevation. For a mounted deck gun, elevation is 0 PSI.

problems.title: Relay Operation Total Flow
problems.question: The relay operation is supplying one attack-engine deck gun with a 1⅜-inch smooth-bore tip operating at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. Use the smooth-bore flow formula: GPM = 29.7 x diameter^2 x √NP. What is the approximate total flow requirement for the relay operation?
problems.instructorExplanation: A 1⅜-inch smooth-bore tip at 80 PSI flows approximately 500 GPM, so that is the relay flow requirement.
problems.explainMistake: This question asks for total flow requirement, not pump pressure. Use the smooth-bore flow formula.
